evidence.title: README (segment 1)
README (segment 2)
evidence.excerpt: ### **The AI Coding Agent's Secret Weapon: Flagging Likely Bugs for Fixing Early On**

evidence.title: README (segment 3)
evidence.excerpt: [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT%2BOpenAI%2FAnthropic%20Rider-blue.svg)](./LICENSE) [![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-Linux%20%7C%20macOS%20%7C%20Windows-blue.svg)](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_bug_scanner) [![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-5.2.19-blue.svg)](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_bug_scanner)

evidence.title: README (segment 4)
evidence.excerpt: ```bash # One command to catch 1000+ bug patterns (always main, cache-busted) curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_bug_scanner/main/install.sh?$(date +%s)" \ | bash -s -- ```

evidence.title: README (segment 5)
evidence.excerpt: Just want it to do everything without confirmations? Live life on the edge with easy-mode to auto-install every dependency, accept all prompts, detect local coding agents, and wire their quality guardrails with zero extra questions:

evidence.title: README (segment 6)
evidence.excerpt: ```bash curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_bug_scanner/main/install.sh?$(date +%s)" \ | bash -s -- --easy-mode ```

evidence.title: README (segment 7)
evidence.excerpt: Note: Windows users must run the installer one-liner from within Git Bash, or use WSL for Windows.

evidence.title: README (segment 8)
evidence.excerpt: **Use machine-readable output in agent contexts.** stdout = data, stderr = diagnostics, exit 0 = success.

evidence.title: Installer can install a `main` runner that fetches modules from `v${UBS_VERSION}`, causing checksum mismatch after post-tag module changes
evidence.excerpt: Installer can install a `main` runner that fetches modules from `v${UBS_VERSION}`, causing checksum mismatch after post-tag module changes (state:closed)
